Leptochilus virgatus, also known as the Chinese angelica tree or Leptochilus heteroclitus, is a type of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptochilus virgatus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Leptochilus virgatus is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine.
